query: SpaceXAI total funding raised 2026 projected income data center burn rate

But investors are being more selective about AI stocks, according to Goldman Sachs Research.

Invest better with The Motley Fool."},{"url":"https://www.bnnbloomberg.ca/business/artificial-intelligence/2026/04/24/at-spacex-ai-is-burning-the-cash-that-starlink-earns/","title":"Elon Musk's SpaceX is burning the cash Starlink earns with AI","content":"While Alphabet and Microsoft have deep operating cash flows, SpaceX is bankrolling its push with revenue from rockets and satellites, leaving it with a cash-burn profile closer to a late-stage startup than a trillion-dollar incumbent.
In 2025, the AI division - home to xAI - accounted for 61% of the consolidated company’s $20.74 billion total capital spending.
Google parent Alphabet, Microsoft, Instagram owner Meta, along with Amazon and Oracle, are set to collectively spend more than $600 billion on AI this year.
While the company is aiming to raise $75 billion in its IPO at a valuation of $1.75 trillion, it may have to return to the markets in a few years if capital spending growth continues to outpace that of revenue.
The gap could widen as analysts put the cost of delivering on the company’s plan to launch a constellation of one million data-center satellites in the trillions of dollars."},{"url":"https://www.constellationr.com/insights/news/spacexs-worth-2-trillion-do-you-believe-space-data-centers-xais-potential","title":"SpaceX's worth $2 trillion: Do you believe in space data centers, xAI's potential? | Constellation Research","content":"CEO Elon Musk is now the first trillionaire.
For 2025, SpaceX reported a loss from operations of $2.59 billion on revenue of $18.67 billion with most of that revenue coming from space operations and Starlink.
The 2025 results tell the tale: Starlink delivered revenue of $11.39 billion with an operating profit of $4.42 billion.
xAI's capex for the first three months of the year was $7.723 billion.
Capital spending for 2025 was also driven by xAI with capex of $12.73 billion compared to $3.83 billion and $4.18 billion, respectively.
We believe SpaceX is uniquely positioned to deploy and operate data centers in orbit that can eventually achieve a lower cost than terrestrial data centers over time.""},{"url":"https://finance.yahoo.com/markets/stocks/articles/spacexs-target-valuation-looming-ipo-175911775.html","title":"SpaceX's Target Valuation For Its Looming IPO Is Now ...","content":"May 29, 2026 — In 2025, SpaceX's revenue rose 33% to $18.7 billion, though it reported a generally accepted accounting principles (GAAP) operating loss of $2.6 ...Read more"}]}

tavily.raw: {"query":"SpaceXAI total funding raised 2026 projected income data center burn rate","follow_up_questions":null,"answer":null,"images":[],"results":[{"url":"https://cryptorank.io/news/feed/52674-xai-losses-spacex-ipo-grok-spending","title":"xAI burned $6.4B last year. SpaceX’s IPO filing shows why the spending is far from over","content":"SpaceX’s IPO filing shows xAI posted a $6.4B operating loss in 2025 on $3.2B revenue while AI capex surged to $7.7B in Q1 2026 (annualized ~$30.8B) as it builds Colossus data centers and plans to scale Grok to multiple‑trillion‑parameter models. Grok had 117M MAUs of 550M combined as of March 2026, SpaceX aims to begin orbital AI compute by 2028 and the combined company targets a potential $1.75T IPO valuation, highlighting heavy ongoing cash burn, uncertain path to profitability and implications for fundraising, adoption and infrastructure bets in the broader crypto and tech ecosystem. The filing reveals that SpaceX intends to scale Grok to “multiple trillions of parameters,” a dramatic increase that the company describes as a “step change in reasoning in depth and overall intelligence.” Achieving that scale will require significantly more compute power, and the filing’s “use of proceeds” section explicitly mentions an “expansion of our AI compute infrastructure.”.","score":0.6122407,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://techcrunch.com/2026/05/20/xai-burned-6-4b-last-year-spacexs-ipo-filing-shows-why-the-spending-is-far-from-over","title":"xAI burned $6.4B last year — SpaceX’s IPO filing shows why the spending is far from over | TechCrunch","content":"Elon Musk’s xAI lost $6.4 billion from operations on just $3.2 billion in revenue in 2025, according to SpaceX’s IPO filings. While AI competitors OpenAI and Anthropic are also eyeing public debuts in 2026, SpaceX’s is expected to be one of the largest in history with a potential $1.75 trillion valuation. In 2024, xAI recorded a loss of $1.56 billion on $2.62 billion in revenue. The jump in revenue from 2024 to 2025 came in large part from “AI solutions and infrastructure revenue” totaling $465 million, which includes $365 million in X and Grok subscription revenue and $88 million in data licensing. Still, SpaceX intends to soldier on with Grok; its next-generation AI is expected to scale to “multiple trillions of parameters,” which the filing describes as a “step change in reasoning in depth and overall intelligence.” It’s an ambitious target, and one that’s now recorded in the audited annals of SEC history.","score":0.5550741,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.bnnbloomberg.ca/business/artificial-intelligence/2026/04/24/at-spacex-ai-is-burning-the-cash-that-starlink-earns","title":"Elon Musk's SpaceX is burning the cash Starlink earns with AI","content":"# At SpaceX, AI is burning the cash that Starlink earns. But the company’s pitch to investors for a potentially historic IPO reveals that its main business will be the same as Big Tech: building artificial intelligence. While Alphabet and Microsoft have deep operating cash flows, SpaceX is bankrolling its push with revenue from rockets and satellites, leaving it with a cash-burn profile closer to a late-stage startup than a trillion-dollar incumbent. SpaceX’s satellite broadband business, Starlink, doubled its operating income last year to US$4.42 billion, easily covering the loss incurred in its space division, which is spending heavily on a new satellite-carrying rocket, showed excerpts of the company’s IPO registration reviewed by Reuters. How Big Can This Revenue Stream Get? | The Motley Fool","content":"**Space Exploration Technologies** (SPCX 1.84%), better known as SpaceX, has three distinct parts of its business -- rocket launches, satellite internet, and the xAI artificial intelligence business. In fact, although xAI was the biggest drag on SpaceX's bottom line in 2025, it's starting to look like 2027 and beyond could be a very different story. Here's how SpaceX's new AI compute business has already more than doubled its revenue, where it could go from here, and why investors should pay attention. Keep in mind that SpaceX's business -- including Starlink, the rocket launches, and xAI -- combined for $18.7 billion in revenue in 2025. Even though Starlink and the rocket business continue to scale rapidly in 2026, this has more than doubled SpaceX's revenue. SpaceX clearly stated in its S-1 that it \"expects to enter into additional similar services contracts for compute capacity with third parties,\" and while this statement was made before the most recent deals, it indicates that this business could be a big part of the company's AI future.","score":0.37154752,"raw_content":null}],"response_time":0.78,"request_id":"1a39428c-bc52-4618-9218-85e399709d11"}
